title: carya ovata subsp. australis
intro: Carya ovata subsp. australis, also known as the eastern black walnut, is a subspecies of tree native to eastern Australia, characterized by its dark, thick bark and edible nuts. The eastern black walnut has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to provide health benefits such as relief from arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as having antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the eastern black walnut is often used in tinctures, teas, and capsules, and is also used in traditional remedies for its potential to treat parasitic infections.
